name: Tree of Life
shortDescription: The Tree of Life is a central mystical symbol in Kabbalah, representing the interconnectedness of all things and the path to divine understanding. It is a map of consciousness and creation, often associated with the Tarot and other esoteric traditions.
description: The Tree of Life, or Etz Chayim, is a foundational concept in Kabbalah, a mystical tradition within Judaism. It serves as a symbolic representation of the process of creation, the structure of the cosmos, and the human psyche. The Tree is composed of ten interconnected spheres, known as Sephirot (singular: Sephira), each representing a different aspect of God's divine attributes or emanations. These Sephirot are interconnected by 22 paths, which are associated with the 22 letters of the Hebrew alphabet and, importantly in the context of Tarot, with the Major Arcana cards. The arrangement of the Sephirot describes a hierarchical structure, starting with Keter (Crown) at the top, representing the ultimate divine source, and descending through Chokmah (Wisdom), Binah (Understanding), Chesed (Mercy), Gevurah (Severity), Tiferet (Beauty), Netzach (Victory), Hod (Splendor), Yesod (Foundation), and finally, Malkuth (Kingdom) at the bottom, representing the material world. The Tree of Life is a complex diagram used for meditation, contemplation, and spiritual growth. It provides a framework for understanding the relationship between the divine and the mundane, and it offers a path for ascending back to the divine source. In Tarot, the Tree of Life provides a valuable framework for understanding the deeper symbolism and interconnectedness of the cards. Each card can be associated with a specific Sephira or path, providing additional layers of meaning and interpretation. The Tree's influence extends to other Western esoteric traditions, including Hermeticism, Alchemy, and the Golden Dawn, where it serves as a central model for understanding the universe and the human condition. Understanding the Kabbalistic Tree of Life is crucial for interpreting the esoteric symbolism within many Tarot decks and related systems.

sources.title: The Chicken Qabalah of Rabbi Lamed Ben Clifford
sources.author: Lon Milo DuQuette
